title: `__lazy_import__` throws a `SystemError` if `globals` is not any dict
author: devdanzin
body:
# Bug report

### Bug description:


The interpreter will raise a `SystemError` when `__lazy_import__` is called with a second positional argument:

```python
>>> __lazy_import__('', 1)
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<python-input-82>", line 1, in <module>
    __lazy_import__('', 1)
    ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~^^^^^^^
SystemError: Objects/dictobject.c:2499: bad argument to internal function
```

Found using [fusil](https://github.com/devdanzin/fusil) by @vstinner.


### CPython versions tested on:

CPython main branch

### Operating systems tested on:

Linux

<!-- gh-linked-prs -->
### Linked PRs
* gh-145086
* gh-145088
<!-- /gh-linked-prs -->

comments.author: johnslavik
comments.body:
This reproduces just as well without any positional arguments:

```pycon
>>> __lazy_import__(name='foo', globals=1)
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<python-input-0>", line 1, in <module>
    __lazy_import__(name='foo', globals=1)
    ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
SystemError: Objects/dictobject.c:2499: bad argument to internal function
```

`globals` isn't validated to be a `dict` or `frozendict` before being passed to `PyDict_GetItemRef` in `builtin___lazy_import___impl`.

comments.author: vstinner
comments.body: I wrote https://github.com/python/cpython/pull/145086 to check *globals* type before calling `PyDict_GetItemRef()` on it.
